detaileddescription.para: You can refer to the below stated example applications for more details on how to use vprocss driver.

sect1.title: main.c
sect1.para: Contains an example on how to use the XVprocss driver directly. This example is the main file for the Video Processing Subsystem.

sect1.title: periph.c
sect1.para: Contains an example on how to use the XVprocss driver directly. This example is the top level resource file that will initialize all system level peripherals.

sect1.title: periph.h
sect1.para: This is header for top level resource file that will initialize all system level peripherals.

sect1.title: system.c
sect1.para: Contains an example on how to use the XVprocss driver directly. This example is the top level resource file that will initialize all system level peripherals.

sect1.title: system.h
sect1.para: This is header for top level resource file that will initialize all system level peripherals.

sect1.title: vpss_example.tcl
sect1.para: Contains a tcl file which automates the process of generating the downloadable bit & elf files from the provided example xsa file.

Example application design source files (contained within "examples/src" folder) are tightly coupled with the video processing subsystem example design available in Vivado Catalogue. To run this tcl
Copy the exported example design xsa file in the "examples" directory of the driver
Launch the xsct terminal
cd into the examples directory
source the tcl file xsct%>sourcevpss_example.tcl
execute the script xsct%>vpss_example<xsa_file_name.xsa>

Script will perform following operations
Create workspace
Create HW project
Create BSP
Create Application Project
Build BSP and Application Project
After the process is complete required files will be available in bit file -> vpss_example.sdk/vpss_example_hw_platform folder elf file -> vpss_example.sdk/vpss_example_design/{Debug/Release} folder

When executed on the board the example application will determine the video processing subsystem topology and set the input and output stream configuration accordingly. Test pattern generator IP is used to generate the input stream. Video Lock Monitor IP will then monitor the output of the subsystem (to vidout) to determine if lock is achieved and present the status (Pass/Fail) on the terminal.
Serial terminal baud rate should be set to 115200 For details, see vpss_example.tcl.
